channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ofcom warns TikTok and YouTube fail to adequately protect children online
item.description: Ofcom has criticised TikTok and YouTube, stating that their content feeds remain unsafe for children despite existing safety measures. The regulator's report highlights that both platforms failed to c...

item.title: US pressures Palestinian envoy to withdraw UN General Assembly vice presidency bid over Gaza tensions
item.description: The Trump administration reportedly pressured the Palestinian ambassador to the United Nations, Riyad Mansour, to drop his candidacy for the vice presidency of the UN General Assembly. According to an...

item.title: Fact Check: Duterte’s ICC Defense Misrepresented by Fake Lawyers on Social Media
item.description: Several viral posts on Facebook falsely claimed that former President Rodrigo Duterte would be represented by United States attorney Elle Woods and Australian lawyer Lily Morrow before the Internation...

item.title: Giants Running Back Cam Skattebo Projects Unrealistic 2,000-Yard Season
item.description: New York Giants running back Cam Skattebo, coming off a promising rookie season in 2025, sparked discussion after projecting a 2,000-yard rushing season for 2026. Skattebo recorded 400 yards on 100 ca...

item.title: UCP Caucus Press Release Delays Review of Forever Canadian Program
item.description: The United Conservative Party (UCP) caucus in Alberta has released a statement prematurely, which has caused delays in the operations of the Forever Canadian review committee. The committee was tasked...

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Compensation Plan Tied to Mars Colony and Space Data Centres
item.description: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk has the potential to significantly increase his compensation if the company achieves ambitious milestones, according to a recent SEC filing. Musk was granted one billion performan...

item.title: White House Signals GOP Should Accept Trump's Leadership Amid Party Divisions
item.description: As the Republican Party experiences growing internal divisions, a senior White House official has urged GOP members to recognize and accept former President Donald Trump's influence over the party. Th...

item.title: US indicts Raul Castro over 1996 shootdown of exile planes
item.description: Former Cuban president Raul Castro, aged 94, has been formally indicted in the United States on multiple counts including conspiracy to kill US nationals, murder, and destruction of aircraft. The char...

item.title: Armed men steal motorcycle in Marikina barangay
item.description: In Marikina City, residents of Barangay Sto. Niño reported an incident where a motorcycle was forcibly taken by armed individuals. The event occurred early in the morning, causing concern among local ...

item.title: Rising Threat of Contract Killings in South Africa
item.description: Thato Molosankwe, a respected community activist in Mahikeng, was tragically gunned down at his home in Lomanyaneng. Known for leading marches against gender-based violence and advocating responsible ...

item.title: CNN panel debates Jeff Bezos's proposal to exempt low-income workers from income tax
item.description: On May 20, 2026, a CNN 'NewsNight' panel debated Amazon founder Jeff Bezos's new tax proposal, which he revealed in an interview with Aaron Ross Sorkin on CNBC. Bezos suggested that the lowest-income ...

item.title: Pakistan awards 23 offshore oil blocks after two decades
item.description: Islamabad: After almost 20 years, Pakistan has officially reopened its offshore oil exploration sector. On Wednesday, the government awarded 23 offshore blocks in the Indus and Makran basins, attracti...

item.title: Psychiatrist Warns of Potential Risk in Trump's Decision-Making During Second Term
item.description: Dr. John Gartner, a psychiatrist and former Johns Hopkins University professor, expressed serious concerns about President Donald Trump's mental state and decision-making capabilities in a recent podc...

item.title: Dana White Reports Insect Infestation in Trump’s Renovated Rose Garden
item.description: Dana White, the CEO of the Ultimate Fighting Championship and longtime supporter of former President Donald Trump, revealed an extensive insect problem in the newly renovated Rose Garden at the White ...

item.title: Parliament Calls for NFC Reform to Address Rapid Population Growth
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Lawmakers from various political parties have stressed urgent reforms in the National Finance Commission (NFC) formula to reduce over-reliance on population as a basis for resource distribu...

item.title: Pakistan steps up diplomacy to mediate US-Iran tensions
item.description: Amid rising tensions between the United States and Iran, Pakistan has intensified its diplomatic efforts to prevent a possible escalation of conflict. Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visited Tehran for...

item.title: Jacqueline Bisset's Iconic Role and Style in 'Bullitt' (1968)
item.description: In the 1968 film 'Bullitt,' Jacqueline Bisset delivers a memorable performance as Cathy, the girlfriend of Steve McQueen’s character, the tough detective Frank Bullitt. Though her role is secondary, B...

item.title: Review: Rami Malek’s Intense Performance in Ira Sachs’ 1980s AIDS Drama
item.description: Ira Sachs’ latest film, The Man I Love, examines gay culture and the impact of HIV during the homophobic climate of 1980s New York under Reagan. The story follows Jimmy George, a charismatic actor rec...

item.title: University of Alberta athletes Abby Guezen and Ryder Rattee win top U Sports honours in 2026
item.description: The University of Alberta achieved a remarkable feat in Canadian university sports as volleyball star Abby Guezen and sprinter Ryder Rattee claimed the top U Sports awards for 2026. Guezen was named f...

item.title: 031 Theatre Festival Showcases a Variety of Family-Friendly Performances
item.description: The fourth annual 031 Theatre Festival returns to Seabrooke's Theatre at DHS from 4 to 14 June, offering a vibrant mix of South African theatre. The festival features comedy, drama, music, and solo pe...
